title: docker-compose error on Mac: No such image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I have set up podman machine on macOS, and exported DOCKER_HOST to point to the podman socket. I am accessing the container registry using a VPN, because otherwise it is not accessible. docker-compose fails to successfully pull and run images specified in the docker-compose.yaml file. I have seen failed pulls frequently with Podman on Mac and Linux, when behind a VPN. In my experience Docker Engine is more resilient in pulling from bad networks. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
0. Use a VPN service to connect to the Internet

1. brew install podman docker-compose

2. podman machine init vm01

3. podman machine start vm01
4. Go to project directory and run docker-compose up

**Describe the results you received:**
Images for the services are pulled until almost complete (99%) and then start to pull again, and again. 
Tried pulling individually with `docker-compose pull web` and then `docker-compose pull db`, which succeeded, but then running `docker-compose up` again starts to pull images once again. 

One example: 

```
mehdi@MacBook wp % docker-compose up

Creating wp_wordpress_1 ... error

ERROR: for wp_wordpress_1  Cannot create container for service wordpress: No such image: docker.io/library/wordpress: image not known

ERROR: for wordpress  Cannot create container for service wordpress: No such image: docker.io/library/wordpress: image not known
ERROR: Encountered errors while bringing up the project.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful pull and run like with Docker

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Podman Machine

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

An update that this happens without a VPN too. It is not a matter of failing to pull. The images are pulled, even more than once. Then, it fails to create containers with an error like: `No such image`
I have had some success though, but it often fails. And this is a big issue. I was finally going to celebrate migration from Docker Desktop to Podman on macOS :(
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@djnotes, apologies, this issue fell off the radar.  Can you still reproduce the issue?  If so, could you share the compose file to reproduce?

Nowadays, I am using Podman Desktop, and Compose seems to be in good company with Podman Desktop. 
Also, just in case, tried to re-produce this issue following the steps above without using Podman Desktop, and it appears that the engine has improved impressively and changing the network or simply using a bad network does not destroy the progress of the image getting downloaded. 
So, I think I am going to close this issue. Feel free to re-open if there are other relevant things to be discussed.
